title: Argentinian Citrus Empanadas
description: A vegan twist on traditional empanadas, filled with a citrusy and chewy filling, perfect for a snack or dessert.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the masarepa and water until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of citrusy filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with argentinian spice blend.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, prepare the chewy filling by mixing together the citrusy filling ingredients.
Once the empanadas are done, let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the empanadas with a side of chewy filling and crumble on top.
